channel.title: The Car Jury, News
channel.description: News and verdicts on the Indian car market from The Car Jury.

item.title: Renault Duster TCe 100 Returns 19.41 km/l ARAI: Class-Leading, On Paper
item.description: Renault has certified the Duster Turbo TCe 100 at 19.41 km/l ARAI, the highest among turbo-petrol mid-size SUVs. Here is how it stacks up.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: New Tata Tiago Facelift: Still The Smart First-Car Pick At Rs 4.69 Lakh?
item.description: Tata Tiago facelift launches at Rs 4.69 lakh ex-showroom with five trims, refreshed cabin and updated petrol-CNG lineup. Here is The Car Jury's verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: May 2026 Sales: Maruti's 43% Stranglehold Tells You Where The Money Is
item.description: Maruti Suzuki took 43% of May 2026 PV sales as the market grew 26% YoY. Tata and Mahindra followed. The Car Jury's take on what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Renault Duster 1.0 Turbo Claims 19.41kpl: The Smart Pick of the Range
item.description: Renault Duster Turbo TCe 100 claims 19.41kpl ARAI, better than the 1.3 turbo. Priced from Rs 10.49 lakh, it is the value play of the range.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Delhi Opens India's First E85 Pump At Rs 82: Cheap Fuel, But Where Are The Cars?
item.description: India's first E85 fuel station opens in Delhi at Rs 82.12 per litre, Rs 20 cheaper than E20 petrol. The catch: almost no car on sale can run it.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Next-Gen Hyundai i20 Teased With Lambo-Style LEDs: India Wait Begins
item.description: Hyundai Brazil has teased the 4th-gen i20 with Y-shaped LED DRLs and a full-width light bar. Here is what it means for the Indian premium hatch.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: May 2026 Sales: Dzire Tops, Fronx Surges, Creta Holds the SUV Line
item.description: Top 10 cars and SUVs in May 2026: Maruti Dzire leads at 24,546 units, Fronx jumps 52%, Hyundai Creta stays strong in the SUV chart.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata EV Discounts June 2026: Harrier EV Cut By Rs 2.75 Lakh, Curvv EV By Rs 3.35 Lakh
item.description: Tata Motors slashes Harrier EV, Curvv EV, Nexon EV and outgoing Punch EV, Tiago EV prices by up to Rs 3.35 lakh in June 2026. The Car Jury verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XUV7XO Diesel AT AWD Mileage Test: Numbers That Justify the Bigger Engine
item.description: We drove the Mahindra XUV7XO Diesel Automatic AWD for 1,500 km across cities, highways and farm roads. Here is the real mileage verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Next-Gen Hyundai i20 Teased: Curved Screens And ADAS Incoming, But Where's The Diesel Answer?
item.description: Hyundai teases the fourth-gen i20 ahead of its global debut next month, with sharper styling, curved displays and likely Level 2 ADAS on the cards.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai bets big on Tamil Nadu: 90% localisation and a mass-market EV by year-end
item.description: Hyundai will make Tamil Nadu its EV hub, push localisation to 90% by 2032, and launch its first mass-market dedicated EV from Chennai this year.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Hyundai Creta Gets Rs 1 Lakh Off In June 2026: Worth Grabbing Before Sierra Lands
item.description: Hyundai is offering up to Rs 1 lakh in benefits on the Creta petrol and N Line in June 2026. Diesel buyers get far less. Here is our take.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Audi Nuvolari revealed: 1,001hp V8 PHEV halo lands at Rs 6.64 crore
item.description: Audi unveils the Nuvolari, a 1,001hp V8 plug-in hybrid halo supercar limited to 499 units, priced at Rs 6.64 crore and based on the Lamborghini Temerario.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Nexon Petrol Gets Rs 55,000 Off In June 2026: Worth Grabbing?
item.description: Tata Nexon petrol variants get benefits up to Rs 55,000 in June 2026. Curvv gets the steepest cuts. Diesel buyers get less. Here is our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Skoda Peaq Sketches Out: Seven-Seat EV Flagship Lands June 23
item.description: Skoda reveals first Peaq design sketches ahead of June 23 debut. The 4,874mm seven-seat EV becomes the brand's largest and priciest model yet.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Toyota Innova Crysta Launched at Rs 19.72 Lakh: Toyota Milks the Cash Cow One More Time
item.description: Toyota has launched the 2026 Innova Crysta facelift at Rs 19.72 lakh with revised styling and cabin updates. The Car Jury's verdict on whether it still makes sense.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti Wagon R Flex-Fuel: India's First E85 Car Is A Policy Win, Not A Buyer's Win
item.description: Maruti showcases the Wagon R flex-fuel, India's first E85-compliant car. Cheaper fuel is promised, but pumps and economics decide if buyers benefit.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Maruti's E100 Flex-Fuel Car Lands June 5: Gadkari Spills The Plan
item.description: Nitin Gadkari confirms Maruti Suzuki will unveil India's first E100 ethanol passenger car on June 5. Wagon R and Fronx are the likely candidates.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Toyota Innova Crysta gets cosmetic update, costlier by up to Rs 83,000
item.description: Toyota launches the 2026 Innova Crysta from Rs 19.72 lakh ex-showroom with mild styling tweaks. Likely the last update before 2027 phase-out.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Toyota Vellfire Updated In Japan: India Launch Makes Business Sense
item.description: Toyota has updated the Vellfire and Alphard in Japan with new colours, trim and ride comfort upgrades. India launch is unconfirmed for now.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra XEV 9E Spied In Australia: Export Push Begins, But India Still Waits On Fixes
item.description: Mahindra XEV 9E spotted testing undisguised in Melbourne signals an Australia launch. The Car Jury on what it means for the India buyer.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Citroen eC3 Facelift Teased: Cosmetic Fixes Won't Solve The Real Problem
item.description: Citroen has teased the eC3 facelift with LED lights, refreshed bumpers and a longer feature list. Mechanicals unchanged. Our verdict on whether it matters.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Nissan Tekton Spied Ahead Of July Launch: A Duster In Sharper Clothes
item.description: Nissan Tekton mid-size SUV spied in final testing ahead of its July 9, 2026 launch. Shares Renault Duster underpinnings with distinct boxy styling.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota GRMN Corolla: 305hp Hot Hatch India Will Never Get
item.description: Toyota Gazoo Racing reveals the 305hp GRMN Corolla with 415Nm, carbonfibre panels and a manual-only gearbox. India is not on the list.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Safari EV Spied: Late To The 7-Seat EV Party Mahindra Already Owns
item.description: Tata Safari EV spotted testing ahead of a festive-season launch, set to take on the Mahindra XEV 9S in the 7-seater electric SUV space.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: BYD's First India PHEV Lands June 9: Atto 2 Or Sealion 6 Incoming
item.description: BYD will launch its first plug-in hybrid for India on June 9, with the Atto 2 DM-i and Sealion 6 DM-i as the likely contenders.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Fuel Price Shock Sends Buyers to Maruti's CNG and e Vitara
item.description: Maruti says e Vitara bookings nearly doubled and CNG demand jumped 40 percent in May 2026 after petrol and diesel went up Rs 8 per litre.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Vingroup's Green SM Taxi Lands In India June 5: A Vinfast Trojan Horse
item.description: Vingroup's Vietnamese ride-hailing arm Green SM launches in Delhi-NCR on June 5, 2026, using only Vinfast cars led by the Limo Green MPV.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: May 2026 PV Sales: Maruti, Mahindra, Tata Shrug Off Fuel Hikes
item.description: Indian PV industry clocks 4.4 lakh units in May 2026 as Maruti, Tata and Mahindra ride CNG and EV demand past fuel price worries.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: May 2026 EV sales: Tata's 10,000-unit month proves the moat is real
item.description: Tata crossed 10,000 EV units in May 2026 as the segment grew 80 percent YoY. The Car Jury on what the numbers say about Tata, Mahindra and MG.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mini Countryman C Lands June 17: The Sensible Countryman Finally Arrives
item.description: Mini confirms the 2026 Countryman C launches in India on June 17, slotting below the JCW All4 with a 1.5-litre turbo-petrol three-cylinder engine.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Tata Safari EV Spied: Flagship Three-Row eSUV Sets Up XEV 9S Fight
item.description: Tata Safari EV spied testing for the first time, expected at Rs 22.5-30 lakh in the festive season. Here is what we spotted and our verdict.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: May 2026 sales: Tata edges Mahindra by 1,069 units, Maruti hits record high
item.description: Tata Motors held second place over Mahindra by just 1,069 units in May 2026 as Maruti Suzuki posted its highest-ever monthly sales.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Kia India's Best-Ever May: 27,586 Units, Up 24% YoY
item.description: Kia India dispatched 27,586 units in May 2026, its highest-ever May, up 23.62% YoY. Seltos continues to anchor the volume story.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW MG Targets 70% Localisation: The Right Fix For The Wrong Problem
item.description: JSW MG Motor India commits Rs 3,700 crore to push localisation to 70 percent across ICE and EVs. Our verdict on what it means for buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: June 2026 Launches: S-Class PHEV Headlines, BMW X4 Returns, Tata Lurks
item.description: June 2026 brings the Mercedes S-Class facelift with a PHEV S 450e, the BMW X4 coupe-SUV's return, and possible Tata EV and BYD PHEV launches.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mahindra's Vision S Slips Out: Pratap Bose's Deleted Story Hints at Production SUV
item.description: Mahindra design boss Pratap Bose briefly posted, then deleted, an Instagram story showing what looks like the production Vision S sub-4m SUV.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Isobutanol-Diesel Blend Mandate Looms: Why Diesel Buyers Should Not Panic
item.description: MoRTH says an isobutanol-diesel blending mandate could roll out in 2026. What it means for Indian diesel car buyers and the brands selling them.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Carmakers Hedge On EVs: Why Less Than Half Of FY26 Launches Are Electric
item.description: Indian automakers are splitting bets across petrol, diesel, hybrid, CNG and EVs in FY26, with less than half of new launches electric. Here is why.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mercedes Recalls G 580 Electric in India Over Loose Wheel Bolt Risk
item.description: Mercedes-Benz India recalls 99 units of the G 580 Electric over wheel bolts that may loosen with use, the brand's second recall this year.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Volkswagen Enters Sri Lanka With Made-in-India Taigun And Virtus
item.description: Skoda Auto Volkswagen India launches the VW brand in Sri Lanka with Chakan-built Taigun and Virtus, opening a 3S facility near Colombo.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: E25 Petrol As India's Default In 2-3 Years: Mileage Will Drop, Prices Won't
item.description: Government is in talks with automakers to make E25 petrol India's standard fuel in 2-3 years. Here is what it means for your car and your wallet.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Mumbai CNG hits Rs 86/kg: second hike in May erodes the fuel's case
item.description: Mahanagar Gas raises Mumbai CNG by Rs 2/kg to Rs 86, the second hike this month. The running-cost gap to petrol keeps narrowing.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Jeep Borrows Tata's ARGOS Platform: India Becomes Jeep's Global Factory
item.description: Stellantis confirms an all-new Jeep SUV on the Tata Sierra's ARGOS platform, built in India for 50-plus markets, launching in 2028.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: SAIC Hands JSW The Keys: What An Indian-Controlled MG Means For Buyers
item.description: SAIC will sell another 10% in JSW MG Motor India to JSW, making the Jindal group the largest shareholder. Here is what it means for MG buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Jeep Teases Mystery SUV: Tata ARGOS-Based India Project Or NG Renegade?
item.description: Stellantis teased a new Jeep SUV at Investor Day 2026. Is it the next-gen Renegade or the Tata ARGOS-based India project? The Car Jury weighs in.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: 2026 Honda City Facelift: Sharper Face, Same Hybrid Smarts, Still The Thinking Buyer's Sedan
item.description: Honda has launched the 2026 City facelift with a new fascia, cabin tweaks and the e:HEV hybrid carried over. Here is our verdict for Indian buyers.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: JSW Takes The Wheel At MG India As SAIC Cuts Stake To 39 Percent
item.description: SAIC is selling another 10 percent of JSW MG Motor India to JSW Group, making the Indian partner the largest shareholder. Here is what it means.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Ebella E3 At Rs 23.60 Lakh: Rs 3.60 Lakh Tax For A Badge
item.description: Toyota Ebella top E3 trim launched at Rs 23.60 lakh, Rs 3.60 lakh more than the identical Maruti e Vitara. The Car Jury's verdict on the badge premium.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)

item.title: Toyota Shelves Lexus LF-ZC EV: A Win For The Hybrid Bet, Not A Surrender
item.description: Toyota has reportedly halted the Lexus LF-ZC electric sedan as global EV demand cools, joining Honda and Nissan in trimming EV plans.
item.author: noreply@thecarjury.com (Rajat Dewasi)
